sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a non-healing wound on the right foot that has been present for approximately 2-3 weeks.
The wound is described as a 1x2 inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the right fifth MTP joint, with yellow slough and minimal granulation tissue present.
The patient reports not feeling any pain or discomfort in the wound.
The patient has a history of diabetes, with an A1C of slightly over 8.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes, and has seen a podiatrist in the past.
The patient has neuropathy, which may have contributed to the development of the wound.
The patient recently completed a course of antibiotics for the wound, which did not lead to improvement.
Review of systems: The patient denies fever, chills, drainage from the wound, or any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's temperature is 98.1, heart rate is 72, respirations are 16, and blood pressure is 110/65.

Physical exam: The patient has a 1x2 inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the right fifth MTP joint, with yellow slough and minimal granulation tissue present.
The wound does not exhibit erythema, cellulitis, or fluid collection, and there is no necrosis or odor.
The patient has palpable bilateral femoral and popliteal pulses, but no palpable DP or PT pulses.
Doppler signs are present.
Test Results: The patient's right foot X-ray shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; suspected impaired circulation; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed collagenase ointment to be applied to the wound once daily, covered with a dry sterile dressing.
I will perform a debridement in the office to remove dead tissue.
I will order an Ankle Brachial Index (ABI) to determine the blood supply to the foot.
I will refer the patient to a vascular specialist if the ABI indicates poor blood supply for wound healing.
I will see the patient again in two weeks.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again in two weeks.
